report_name: Supply Chain Attack: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI Provider API Keys from Developers

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor distributed at least 15 malicious plugins through the legitimate JetBrains Marketplace, masquerading them as AI coding assistants to compromise developer environments.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The malicious plugins acted as a deceptive password store, tricking developers into entering their AI service API keys into the plugin's settings within their IDE, which were then stolen.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Stolen API keys were exfiltrated from the compromised IDEs to an attacker-controlled C2 server using unencrypted HTTP POST requests.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The malicious plugins sent stolen API keys in a plaintext JSON payload over an HTTP C2 channel to the attacker's server.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses
attack_patterns.description: Some of the malicious plugins installed a custom trust manager to suppress TLS certificate validation errors, bypassing security warnings during their operation.

malware.name: Malicious JetBrains AI Plugins
malware.description: A collection of at least 15 malicious plugins on the JetBrains Marketplace that masqueraded as AI coding assistants to steal API keys for services like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google AI from developers' IDEs.

identities.name: JetBrains
identities.description: Vendor of the JetBrains Marketplace, which was compromised by the distribution of malicious plugins. The company has since removed the plugins and is enhancing its security.

identities.name: OpenAI
identities.description: AI service provider whose API keys were targeted and stolen by the malicious JetBrains plugins.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: AI service provider whose API keys were targeted and stolen by the malicious JetBrains plugins.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: Parent company of Google AI, an AI service whose API keys were targeted and stolen by the malicious JetBrains plugins.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Plugin C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A hardcoded command-and-control server at IP 39.107.60.51 used to receive stolen API keys from the malicious JetBrains plugins via unencrypted HTTP POST requests.

indicators.name: Malicious JetBrains Plugin C2 IP
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious JetBrains Plugin C2 Connection
id: 2b8c1f9a-3e4d-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections to an IP address known to be a C2 server for malicious JetBrains plugins stealing AI API keys.
references:
  - https://diesec.com/2026/06/top-5-cybersecurity-news-stories-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp: '39.107.60.51'
    Initiated: 'true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the C2 server used by malicious JetBrains plugins.

indicators.name: Suspicious Unencrypted HTTP POST from Java Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Unencrypted HTTP POST from Java Process
id: 8d7e6f5a-4b3c-4a2b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects unencrypted HTTP POST requests originating from a Java process, which could indicate exfiltration by a malicious JetBrains plugin.
references:
  - https://diesec.com/2026/06/top-5-cybersecurity-news-stories-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\java.exe'
    DestinationPort: 80
    Protocol: 'tcp'
    Initiated: 'true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects behavior consistent with the malicious plugins' exfiltration method, where unencrypted HTTP POST requests are sent from the underlying Java process of the IDE.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Unencrypted HTTP POST from Java Process
